title: High Recall AND High Precision in Search
problem_statement:
A legal research platform indexes 340 million court documents, statutes, regulations, and legal analyses. Attorneys use the platform for case research where missing a relevant precedent (false negative) can lead to malpractice liability, while surfacing irrelevant results (false positives) wastes billable time at $400-800/hour.

The current BM25 + semantic re-ranking pipeline achieves 91% recall at 34% precision (returning ~180 results per query, of which ~60 are relevant). Tightening ranking thresholds to achieve 85% precision drops recall to 67%, potentially missing critical precedents.

The system must return every relevant document (100% recall target) while presenting only relevant documents (>90% precision target) — requirements that are mathematically opposed in information retrieval theory for any single ranking function over a heterogeneous corpus.

ground_truth.ifr_baseline: Every relevant document is found and presented while zero irrelevant results appear
